tools.description: Append a non-destructive comment/message to one activity when write tools are enabled. This tool only adds a new message and never overwrites prior messages.

activity_id.description: Required intervals.icu activity ID to append a message/comment to. Surrounding whitespace is trimmed.

include_full.description: When true, include the raw upstream add-message response under full; default returns a terse append confirmation.

message.description: Required free-text message/comment content. Must be non-empty after trimming; the body is sent as content and is not logged by icuvisor.

tools.name: add_activity_message

outputSchema.description: Append-only activity message confirmation with activity_id, message_id, status, and normalized athlete_id metadata when available.

tools.description: Create or update a non-destructive calendar event such as a planned workout, race, or note. Omitting event_id creates a new event; providing event_id updates that event without deleting or replacing unrelated events.

examples.description: Keep the day flexible; optional mobility only.
examples.name: Travel day

examples.description: B race. Practice breakfast, warm-up, and even pacing.
examples.name: 10K tune-up race

input_examples.description: Keep the day flexible; optional mobility only.
input_examples.name: Travel day

input_examples.description: B race. Practice breakfast, warm-up, and even pacing.
input_examples.name: 10K tune-up race

category.description: Required upstream event category. Documented upstream categories include WORKOUT, RACE_A, RACE_B, RACE_C, NOTE, PLAN, HOLIDAY, SICK, INJURED, SET_EFTP, FITNESS_DAYS, SEASON_START, TARGET, SET_FITNESS; see icuvisor://event-categories. Custom athlete/account category values are passed through.

date.description: Required athlete-local event date as YYYY-MM-DD; interpreted in the configured athlete timezone.

description.description: Optional free-text athlete or coach notes. Preserved verbatim, including whitespace and line breaks; mutually exclusive with workout_doc.

distance_meters.description: Optional planned distance in meters when supported upstream.

elapsed_time_seconds.description: Optional planned elapsed duration in seconds when supported upstream.

event_id.description: Optional upstream event ID to update. Omit to create a new event; this tool never deletes events.

include_full.description: When true, include the raw upstream event payload under event.full; default response matches get_event_by_id's terse event shape.

moving_time_seconds.description: Optional planned moving duration in seconds when supported upstream.

name.description: Optional event title/name shown on the athlete calendar.

tags.description: Optional event tags to preserve on the upstream event, in caller-provided order.

target_load.description: Optional planned training load / TSS equivalent when supported upstream.

type.description: Required for WORKOUT events: upstream sport/activity type such as Ride, Run, Swim, or the athlete account's configured activity type. Surrounding whitespace is trimmed.

workout_doc.description: Optional structured WorkoutDoc. Mutually exclusive with description; serialized to the upstream workout DSL. Syntax reference: icuvisor://workout-syntax.

tools.name: add_or_update_event

outputSchema.description: Write confirmation containing the same terse event row shape used by get_event_by_id plus operation/date/timezone metadata.

tools.description: List activities for a date range with terse unit-disambiguated rows, Strava-unavailable detection, and opaque pagination. Use this before details, intervals, streams, splits, or messages when a prompt asks about recent training.

include_full.description: When true, include raw upstream activity fields and preserve upstream nulls; default terse rows are unit-disambiguated and null-stripped.

include_unnamed.description: When false, drop rows with an empty activity name after bounded pagination.

newest.description: Optional local ISO-8601 end date/date-time; defaults upstream to now.

next_page_token.description: Opaque token from _meta.next_page_token for the next page. Do not edit.

oldest.description: Required local ISO-8601 start date/date-time unless next_page_token is supplied.

page_size.description: Number of terse rows to return per page; values above 200 are capped.

tools.name: get_activities

outputSchema.description: Paginated activities with unit-disambiguated terse rows, Strava unavailable markers, and _meta.next_page_token when more data may be available.

tools.description: Get one activity's terse metadata and metrics by activity_id. Use include_full only when raw upstream fields are needed; Strava-blocked activities return an unavailable marker instead of sparse N/A rows.

include_full.description: Include raw upstream fields; default terse mode strips nulls and returns normalized fields.

tools.name: get_activity_details

tools.description: Get analyzed intervals for one activity by activity_id. Interval units are normalized to the canonical intervals.icu unit enum and raw interval payloads require include_full.

include_full.description: Include raw upstream fields; default terse mode strips nulls and returns normalized fields.

tools.name: get_activity_intervals

tools.description: List comments and notes on one activity by activity_id. Responses are terse by default and render created timestamps in the athlete's configured timezone.

activity_id.description: intervals.icu activity ID whose messages/comments should be listed.

include_full.description: Include raw upstream message fields and preserve nulls.

limit.description: Maximum messages to return; defaults to 100 and is capped at 200.

since_id.description: Return messages after this upstream message ID; zero means no cursor.

tools.name: get_activity_messages

tools.description: Get manual or virtual per-km/per-mile activity splits. Uses manual intervals when present, otherwise derives virtual splits from distance/time streams and honors preferred_units.

activity_id.description: Required intervals.icu activity ID whose manual or virtual splits should be returned.

include_full.description: When true, preserve full response metadata during shaping; split rows remain terse and unit-disambiguated by default.

split_unit.description: Optional split distance unit. Defaults to the athlete's preferred_units when omitted, falling back to km.

tools.name: get_activity_splits

tools.description: Get the configured athlete profile, FTP/thresholds, zones, and sport settings from intervals.icu. Use this for athlete identity, units, timezone, FTP, heart-rate thresholds, pace thresholds, and zone configuration; do not use it for activities, wellness, fitness trends, events, or workouts.

include_full.description: When true, include additional typed, non-secret profile and sport-setting identifiers. Defaults to false; raw upstream payloads and credentials are never returned.

tools.name: get_athlete_profile

outputSchema.description: Terse athlete profile with normalized athlete_id, units, timezone, sport thresholds/zones, and _meta.server_version.

tools.description: Get upstream best efforts grouped by sport and default/requested power, heart-rate, and pace buckets. Defaults to Ride, Run, and Swim with terse bucket rows.

distance_meters.description: Pace distance buckets. Defaults depend on sport: run-style 400,1000,1609,5000,10000m; swim 50,100,200,400,1500m.

duration_seconds.description: Power/HR duration buckets. Defaults to 5,15,30,60,300,1200,3600 seconds.

include_full.description: When true, include raw upstream curve arrays and activity maps.

newest.description: Optional local end date YYYY-MM-DD. Supply with oldest or omit both for all-time.

oldest.description: Optional local start date YYYY-MM-DD. Supply with newest or omit both for all-time.

sports.description: Intervals.icu sports/types to fan out. Defaults to Ride, Run, Swim.

tools.name: get_best_efforts

outputSchema.description: Best efforts grouped by sport and bucket.

tools.description: Fetch a single calendar event detail by event_id. If the detail endpoint 404s, performs one bounded list scan and returns a structured unavailable result instead of exposing the raw 404.

date.description: Optional athlete-local YYYY-MM-DD date hint. On detail 404, scans ±30 days around this date.

event_id.description: Required upstream event ID. Numeric upstream IDs should be passed as strings.

include_full.description: When true, include the raw upstream event payload under event.full; default response is terse.

newest.description: Optional athlete-local scan end YYYY-MM-DD. Must be paired with oldest and span at most 61 days.

oldest.description: Optional athlete-local scan start YYYY-MM-DD. Must be paired with newest and span at most 61 days.

resolve.description: Optional upstream resolve flag for the fallback list scan; defaults to true to recover recurring or derived event instances.

tools.name: get_event_by_id

outputSchema.description: One calendar event as {event,_meta}, or a structured non-error {unavailable,_meta} result when detail 404 plus bounded list scan cannot find the ID.

tools.description: List calendar events across a bounded athlete-local YYYY-MM-DD date range. Returns terse rows by default, raw upstream event payloads only with include_full:true, and preserves upstream category enum values.

category.description: Optional upstream event category filter. Documented upstream categories include WORKOUT, RACE_A, RACE_B, RACE_C, NOTE, PLAN, HOLIDAY, SICK, INJURED, SET_EFTP, FITNESS_DAYS, SEASON_START, TARGET, SET_FITNESS; see icuvisor://event-categories. Custom athlete/account category values are passed through.

include_full.description: When true, include each raw upstream event payload under full; default rows are terse and null-stripped.

limit.description: Maximum event rows to request; defaults to 100 and values above 500 are capped.

newest.description: Required athlete-local end date YYYY-MM-DD; date range is capped at 366 days.

resolve.description: Optional upstream resolve flag for recurring/derived events when supported by intervals.icu.

tools.name: get_events

outputSchema.description: Bounded calendar event rows with raw upstream category enum values, date-range metadata, truncation metadata, and optional full raw payloads.

tools.description: Get CTL, ATL, and TSB fitness trends for a local date range. Dates are athlete-local YYYY-MM-DD values.

end_date.description: local end date as YYYY-MM-DD in the athlete timezone.

include_full.description: When true, include raw upstream summary rows.

start_date.description: local start date for fitness rows as YYYY-MM-DD in the athlete timezone.

tools.name: get_fitness

outputSchema.description: Fitness rows with CTL, ATL, and TSB.

tools.description: Get aggregated training volume, neutral training load, sRPE, and upstream zone-order totals for a local date range.

end_date.description: local end date as YYYY-MM-DD in the athlete timezone.

include_full.description: When true, include raw upstream summary rows.

start_date.description: local start date for summary rows as YYYY-MM-DD in the athlete timezone.

tools.name: get_training_summary

tools.description: Get daily wellness rows for a local date range with distinct sleepQuality, sleepScore, sleepSecs, custom fields, and native provider sidecars. Dates are athlete-local YYYY-MM-DD values.

fields.description: Optional upstream wellness field names to request; custom fields are preserved when returned.

include_full.description: When true, include the raw upstream wellness row under full and keep null fields.

newest.description: Local newest wellness date YYYY-MM-DD in the athlete timezone, inclusive.

oldest.description: Local oldest wellness date YYYY-MM-DD in the athlete timezone.

tools.name: get_wellness_data

outputSchema.description: Daily wellness rows with distinct sleepQuality (1-4), sleepScore (0-100), sleepSecs, custom fields, and _native provider fields.

tools.description: Discover tools hidden from the default core catalog and explain how to enable the full icuvisor toolset. This tool makes no intervals.icu API calls.
tools.name: icuvisor_list_advanced_capabilities

outputSchema.description: Full-only icuvisor tools and instructions for enabling them.

tools.description: Manually pair one completed activity with one planned calendar event when intervals.icu auto-pairing misses (forum #97). This is a non-destructive write: it sets the activity's paired_event_id and does not delete activities or events.

activity_id.description: Required intervals.icu activity ID to pair. Surrounding whitespace is trimmed; IDs are otherwise preserved exactly.

event_id.description: Required numeric intervals.icu planned event ID to set as the activity paired_event_id. Surrounding whitespace is trimmed.

include_full.description: When true, include the raw upstream updated activity payload under full; default returns a terse link confirmation.

tools.name: link_activity_to_event

outputSchema.description: Non-destructive activity/event link confirmation with activity_id, event_id, status, and _meta.warnings when the activity/event dates differ.

tools.description: Update one athlete-local wellness row with sparse manual fields: subjective scales, measurements, injury text, and locked; device-owned sleepScore and _native bridge fields are not writable.

examples.injury: Mild calf tightness after hills; keep run easy.

input_examples.injury: Mild calf tightness after hills; keep run easy.

bloodGlucose.description: Manual blood glucose in the upstream intervals.icu wellness unit.

date.description: Required athlete-local wellness date as YYYY-MM-DD.

diastolic.description: Manual diastolic blood pressure in mmHg.

include_full.description: When true, include the raw upstream wellness row under wellness.full and keep null fields.

injury.description: Optional free-text injury or limitation note. Preserved verbatim.

locked.description: When true, ask upstream to lock the wellness row against device-sync overwrites.

restingHR.description: Manual resting heart rate in bpm.

systolic.description: Manual systolic blood pressure in mmHg.

weight.description: Manual body weight in the athlete's preferred weight unit from get_athlete_profile (_meta.units / units.weight); converted to upstream kg at the API boundary.

tools.name: update_wellness

outputSchema.description: Updated wellness row using the same terse read shape as get_wellness_data, plus write metadata and delete-mode/unit metadata.
